description: HP 8970B Noise Figure Meter (10-1600 MHz, extendable to 26.5 GHz with an 8971B/8972A test set and a system LO). Legacy HP-IB (IEEE-488) instrument using a two-letter alphameric program-code language, NOT SCPI. Almost every function is reachable two ways: a dedicated HP-IB code (e.g. FR, CA, W1) or a Special Function entered as a code number followed by SP (e.g. 1.0SP, 13.2SP). Numeric data is entered Function-Data-Units (e.g. FR100MZ); units codes are HZ and MZ for frequency, DB for dB, etc. The ENTER code is EN. Letters may be upper or lower case; characters other than 0-9, '.', '-' and the two valid code letters are ignored. The instrument talks and listens by default: each time it is addressed to talk it returns the current measurement as a fixed 12-byte real constant +/-DDDDDE+/-NN<CR><LF> (sign, five mantissa digits with the decimal point assumed right of the 5th digit, 'E', signed exponent), in fundamental units (Hz, dB, K). Output content is selected by H0/H1/H2 (43.0/43.1/43.2). Errors are output in the same 12-byte format as +90DDDE+06 where DD is the error code; +90000E+06 means data-not-ready. CALIBRATE (CA) and AUTO/SINGLE sweep (W1/W2) do NOT toggle over the bus - use W0 to turn sweep/calibrate off remotely. A Device Clear (DCL/SDC) or PRESET (PR) returns the instrument to Mode 1.0 defaults. There is no instrument-ID query (no *IDN); SD only shows the software date on the front-panel display.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: FREQUENCY key. Sets the fixed tuned frequency. Enter value in MHz (HP-IB inputs are rounded to the nearest MHz). Range 10-1600 MHz in Mode 1.0/1.5/1.9 (2047 MHz opt 020), 10-26500 MHz with test set, 10-99999 MHz in Modes 1.1-1.3/1.6-1.8. Terminate with MZ (MHz) or HZ (Hz); all values are taken as the unit shown. Default 30 MHz (Mode 1.0).

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 10 to 1600 MHz (up to 26500/99999 MHz depending on mode/test set)

commands.name: start_frequency
commands.description: START FREQ key (Special Function not used). Sets the sweep start frequency. Default 10 MHz.

commands.name: stop_frequency
commands.description: STOP FREQ key. Sets the sweep stop frequency.

commands.name: step_size
commands.description: STEP SIZE key. Sets the frequency step (increment) between measurement points in a sweep.

commands.name: frequency_increment
commands.description: FREQ INCR key. Sets the fixed-frequency tuning increment used by the UP/DN step keys. Enter integer MHz (decimals are rounded to nearest MHz).

commands.name: step_frequency_up
commands.description: Steps the fixed tuned frequency up by the current frequency increment (set with FN).

commands.name: step_frequency_down
commands.description: Steps the fixed tuned frequency down by the current frequency increment (set with FN).

commands.name: enter
commands.description: ENTER key. Completes a numeric data entry (equivalent of pressing ENTER). Used to terminate values where a units code is not supplied, and to separate frequency/ENR pairs during ENR table entry.

commands.name: auto_sweep
commands.description: AUTO key. Starts a repetitive (auto) sweep from start to stop frequency. Does NOT toggle over the bus - use W0 to stop. To guarantee the sweep starts at the start frequency, send FA then W1.

commands.name: single_sweep
commands.description: SINGLE key. Performs one single sweep from start to stop frequency. Does NOT toggle over the bus.

commands.name: sweep_off
commands.description: Turns sweep and calibrate off. Must be used to turn AUTO/SINGLE sweep or CALIBRATE off over the HP-IB, since those functions do not toggle remotely.

commands.name: measurement_mode
commands.description: Selects the measurement mode (Special Function 1.0-1.9). E0=1.0 (10-1600 MHz direct), E1=1.1 (fixed IF, variable-freq system LO), E2=1.2 (variable IF, fixed-freq system LO, SSB), E3=1.3 (fixed IF, variable-freq sys LO, mixer is DUT), E4=1.4 (variable IF, fixed-freq sys LO, mixer is DUT), E5=1.5 (10-26500 MHz w/ test set), E6=1.6 (fixed IF, variable user-controlled LO), E7=1.7 (variable IF, fixed user LO, SSB), E8=1.8 (fixed IF, variable user LO, mixer is DUT), E9=1.9 (variable IF, fixed user LO, mixer is DUT). Mode 1.0 is the PRESET default.

commands.name: measurement_noise_figure_uncorrected
commands.description: NOISE FIGURE (UNCORRECTED) measurement. Measures uncorrected noise figure (no second-stage/cal correction).

commands.name: measurement_noise_figure_and_gain
commands.description: NOISE FIGURE AND GAIN (CORRECTED) measurement. Measures corrected noise figure and gain using the calibration data.

commands.name: calibrate
commands.description: CALIBRATE key. Performs the second-stage (system) calibration over the swept range. Does NOT toggle over the bus - use W0 to abort/turn off.

commands.name: cold_calibration_manual
commands.description: Cold Calibration for a manual measurement (Special Function 14.3) - noise source OFF.

commands.name: hot_calibration_manual
commands.description: Hot Calibration for a manual measurement (Special Function 14.4) - noise source ON.

commands.name: cold_measurement_manual
commands.description: Cold Manual Measurement (Special Function 14.1) - noise source OFF.

commands.name: hot_measurement_manual
commands.description: Hot Manual Measurement (Special Function 14.2) - noise source ON.

commands.name: display_current_measurement
commands.description: Normal Display (Special Function 15.0) - returns from displaying manual-measurement results to the current measurement.

commands.name: display_manual_measurement_results
commands.description: Display Manual Measurement Results (Special Function 15.1).

commands.name: sideband_offset
commands.description: Sideband Frequency Offset (Special Function 2.0-2.3). B0=2.0 Double Sideband (no offset), B1=2.1 Lower Single Sideband (Fsignal<FLO), B2=2.2 Upper Single Sideband (Fsignal>FLO), B3=2.3 Signal Up Conversion (F_IF=Fsignal+FLO).

commands.name: if_frequency
commands.description: Enter IF frequency (Special Function 3.0) for Modes 1.1, 1.3, 1.6 and 1.8.

commands.name: lo_frequency
commands.description: Enter LO frequency (Special Function 3.1) for Modes 1.2, 1.4, 1.7 and 1.9.

commands.name: display_user_lo_frequency
commands.description: Displays the User-Controlled LO frequency (Special Function 3.2) in Modes 1.6 through 1.9.

commands.name: use_enr_table
commands.description: Use ENR Table (Special Function 5.0). Selects the stored frequency-dependent ENR table for measurements/calibration.

commands.name: use_spot_enr
commands.description: Use Spot ENR (Special Function 5.1). Uses a single spot ENR value rather than the table.

commands.name: display_current_enr
commands.description: Display Current ENR in dB (Special Function 5.2).

commands.name: enter_spot_enr
commands.description: Enter and Use Spot ENR (Special Function 5.3). Follow with the spot ENR value in dB and EN. Default 15.2 dB.

commands.name: enter_enr_table
commands.description: ENR key / Enter ENR Table. Enters ENR table edit mode, then accepts up to 35 frequency(MHz)/ENR(dB) pairs each separated by EN, and is terminated by sending FR to end the entry. The table improves measurement accuracy across frequency.

commands.name: enter_tcold
commands.description: Enter Tcold (Special Function 6.0). Sets the noise-source cold temperature. Default 296.5 K.

commands.name: enter_thot
commands.description: Enter and Use Thot (Special Function 5.4). Sets the noise-source hot temperature.

commands.name: enter_noise_source_id
commands.description: Enter Noise Source Identifier (Special Function 5.5). Enters the noise-source serial/ID number.

commands.name: noise_source_catalog
commands.description: Noise Source Catalog (Special Function 5.6).

commands.name: enr_table_for_calibration
commands.description: Selects the ENR table number used for Calibration (Special Function 5.7). Default table 0.

commands.name: enr_table_for_measurement
commands.description: Selects the ENR table number used for Measurement (Special Function 5.8). Default table 0.

commands.name: default_enr
commands.description: Sets all ENR values to 15.20 dB and the noise-source ID number to 00000 (Special Function 95.6).

commands.name: noise_figure_units
commands.description: Noise Figure Display Units (Special Function 10.0-10.4). N0=FdB, N1=F (ratio), N2=YdB, N3=Y (ratio), N4=Te in K.

commands.name: noise_source_temperature_units
commands.description: Selects Noise Source Temperature units for data input (Special Function 11.0-11.2). D0=K, D1=degrees C, D2=degrees F.

commands.name: display_resolution
commands.description: Display Resolution (Special Function 12.0-12.2). X0=Maximum resolution, X1=Less resolution on noise figure, X2=Less resolution on gain.

commands.name: smoothing_type
commands.description: Smoothing (averaging) type (Special Function 13.0-13.1). V0=Exponential smoothing, V1=Arithmetic smoothing.

commands.name: smoothing_factor
commands.description: Enter Smoothing Factor (Special Function 13.2). Sets the averaging factor (number of measurements averaged). Equivalent fixed codes F0-F9 set factor 1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128,256,512.

commands.name: smoothing_factor_fixed
commands.description: Direct smoothing-factor codes. F0=1, F1=2, F2=4, F3=8, F4=16, F5=32, F6=64, F7=128, F8=256, F9=512.

commands.name: increase_smoothing
commands.description: INCREASE key. Increases the smoothing (averaging) factor by one step.

commands.name: decrease_smoothing
commands.description: DECREASE key. Decreases the smoothing (averaging) factor by one step.

commands.name: display_control
commands.description: Display Control (Special Function 16.0-16.3). DF=show measurement frequency in left display, DI=show input frequency (to NFM in Modes 1.0-1.4 or to test set in Modes 1.5-1.9), BF=blank left display, BA=blank all displays.

parameters.name: selection
parameters.range: DF=freq, DI=input, BF=blank left, BA=blank all

commands.name: hpib_data_output
commands.description: HP-IB Data Output Selection (Special Function 43.0-43.2). H0=NOISE FIGURE only output; H1=Frequency(left display), INSERTION GAIN and NOISE FIGURE output; H2=during calibration output Frequency, calibration data (GmKBm) and second-stage temperature. Each output value is the fixed 12-byte +/-DDDDDE+/-NN CR/LF format; EOI is set with each LF.

commands.name: loss_compensation
commands.description: Loss Compensation (Special Function 34.0-34.4). L0=Off, L1=On, LA=Enter loss before DUT in dB, LB=Enter loss after DUT in dB, LT=Enter temperature of losses. Enter LA/LB/LT values terminated with EN.
commands.set: L0 | L1 | LA <dB> EN | LB <dB> EN | LT <temp> EN

commands.name: trigger_selection
commands.description: Trigger Selection (Special Function 30.0-30.2). T0=Free Run, T1=Hold, T2=Execute (single trigger; also triggered by the GET bus command).

commands.name: rf_attenuation
commands.description: RF Attenuation Selection (Special Function 60.0-60.6). R0=Auto, R1=+20 dB, R2=+10 dB, R3=0 dB, R4=-10 dB, R5=-20 dB, R6=-30 dB.

commands.name: display_rf_attenuator
commands.description: Display RF Attenuator setting (Special Function 61.0).

commands.name: rf_attenuator_hold
commands.description: RF Attenuator Hold (Special Function 62.0). Holds the RF attenuators in the configuration that exists when activated.

commands.name: if_attenuation
commands.description: IF Attenuation Selection (Special Function 70.0-70.8). I0=Auto, I1=0 dB, I2=5 dB, I3=10 dB, I4=15 dB, I5=20 dB, I6=25 dB, I7=30 dB, I8=35 dB.

parameters.name: attenuation
parameters.range: I0=Auto, I1=0 ... I8=35 dB (5 dB steps)

commands.name: display_if_attenuator
commands.description: Display IF Attenuator setting (Special Function 71.0).

commands.name: if_attenuator_hold
commands.description: IF Attenuator Hold (Special Function 72.0). Holds the IF attenuators in the configuration that exists when activated.

commands.name: input_gain_calibration
commands.description: Input Gain Calibration range (Special Function 32.0-32.3). C0=+20/+10/0 dB, C1=+10/0/-10 dB, C2=0/-10/-20 dB, C3=-10/-20/-30 dB.

commands.name: frequency_calibration
commands.description: Frequency Calibration (Special Function 31.0-31.2). Y0=Automatic, Y1=Disable frequency cal, Y2=Perform one frequency cal.

commands.name: interpolated_measurements
commands.description: Interpolated Measurements (Special Function 39.0-39.1). DG=Disable interpolated measurements in Modes 1.5-1.9, EG=Enable interpolated measurements in Modes 1.5-1.9.

commands.name: power_measurement
commands.description: Power Measurements (Special Function 9.1-9.4). N5=Source Off (uncal), N6=Source On (uncal), N7=Source Off (cal), N8=Source On (cal).

commands.name: voltmeter_mode_source_off
commands.description: Voltmeter Mode with Noise Source Off (Special Function 80.0).

commands.name: voltmeter_mode_source_on
commands.description: Voltmeter Mode with Noise Source On (Special Function 81.0).

commands.name: oscilloscope_output
commands.description: Output to Oscilloscope (Special Function 7.0-7.5). A0=Noise Figure and Gain, A1=Test Pattern, A2=Noise Figure Only, A3=Gain Only, A8=Cursor enabled, A9=Cursor disabled.

parameters.name: selection
parameters.range: A0=NF+Gain, A1=test pattern, A2=NF, A3=Gain, A8/A9=cursor on/off

commands.name: oscilloscope_limit
commands.description: Enter Oscilloscope Limits (Special Function 8.1-8.4). NL=Noise Figure Lower Limit, NU=Noise Figure Upper Limit, GL=Gain Lower Limit, GU=Gain Upper Limit. Follow with value and EN.
commands.set: NL <value> EN | NU <value> EN | GL <value> EN | GU <value> EN

commands.name: plot_noise_figure
commands.description: Recorder/plot output. A4=Plot Noise Figure (Special Function 22.0), A5=Plot Gain (Special Function 23.0), A6=X-axis Noise Figure / Y-axis Gain strip-chart mode (Special Function 24.0).

parameters.name: selection
parameters.range: A4=plot NF, A5=plot gain, A6=strip chart

commands.name: recorder_go_lower_left
commands.description: Go to Lower Left (Special Function 20.0) for X-Y recorder pen positioning.

commands.name: recorder_go_upper_right
commands.description: Go to Upper Right (Special Function 21.0) for X-Y recorder pen positioning.

commands.name: plotter_function
commands.description: Plotter Functions (Special Function 25.0-25.5). PA=Plot Grid and Data, PG=Plot Grid and Axes Labels, PD=Plot Data Only, NP=Select Noise Pen Number, GP=Select Gain Pen Number, WT=Enter Plot Name.
commands.set: PA | PG | PD | NP <n> EN | GP <n> EN | WT <name>

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: pen number or plot name where applicable

commands.name: store
commands.description: STORE key. Stores the complete instrument configuration into one of ten registers (0-9). Follow with a single-digit register number.

commands.name: recall
commands.description: RECALL key. Recalls the instrument configuration from one of ten registers (0-9). Follow with a single-digit register number.

commands.name: sequence
commands.description: SEQ key. Recalls storage registers 1 through 9 in a preset sequence (used with Special Function 35).

commands.name: sequence_functions
commands.description: Sequence Functions (Special Function 35.0-35.3). QM=Manual, QA=Automatic, QS=Set, QC=Clear.

commands.name: preset
commands.description: PRESET key. Returns the instrument to the known preset state (Mode 1.0 defaults). Equivalent to a Device Clear.

commands.name: special_function
commands.description: SPECIAL FUNCTION key. Terminates entry of a Special Function code number, activating that function. Most special functions can be programmed either as <code-number>SP or by the dedicated two-letter HP-IB code. Refer to the special-function table for code numbers (e.g. 1.0=Mode 1.0, 13.2=smoothing factor, 44.x=SRQ enables).

commands.name: initialize_special_functions
commands.description: Initialize Special Functions (Special Function 0.0). Initializes many special functions. (Special Function 0.9, entered as 0.9SP, initializes or sets defaults for all special functions to factory conditions.)

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Service Request enables (Special Function 44.0-44.8). Q0=Disable SRQ capability (clears all enabled conditions), Q1=Data Ready, Q2=Cal Complete, Q3=HP-IB Code Error, Q4=SRQ on SIB, Q5=NFM received control on SIB, Q6=Instrument Error, Q8=Extended Status Byte. RM (44.7) and RE (44.9) set the status-byte and extended-status-byte masks.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.range: Q0=disable, Q1=data ready, Q2=cal complete, Q3=code error, Q4=SIB SRQ, Q5=received control, Q6=instr error, Q8=ext status

commands.name: set_status_byte_mask
commands.description: Set Status Byte Mask (Special Function 44.7). Enables the desired conditions in the main Status Byte to cause an SRQ. Follow with the mask value and EN.

commands.name: set_extended_status_byte_mask
commands.description: Set Extended Status Byte Mask (Special Function 44.9). Enables the desired conditions in the Extended Status Byte. Follow with the mask value and EN.

commands.name: output_both_status_bytes
commands.description: Output both Status Bytes. The next read returns the main Status Byte and the Extended Status Byte.

commands.name: reset_status_bytes
commands.description: Reset Status Bytes. Clears the main and extended status bytes.

commands.name: recall_last_error
commands.description: Recalls the last error issued (Special Function 99.1). Outputs the error in the 12-byte error format.

commands.name: software_date
commands.description: Displays the current software date on the front-panel display (Special Function 99.9).

commands.name: special_function_catalog
commands.description: Special Function Catalog (Special Function 50.0-50.8). G0=scan catalog lines, G1-G8=display line 1 through line 8 status. With HP-IB output set appropriately, G1-G8 can be read one line at a time.

commands.name: system_lo_program
commands.description: System LO Program selection (Special Function 41.0-41.5). J0=HP 8350B Sweep Oscillator, J2=HP 8671B/8672A Syn. Signal Generator, J3=HP 8673B/C/G Syn. Signal Generator, J4=HP 8340B/8341B Sweep Oscillator, J5=Custom Local Oscillator.

commands.name: system_lo_command
commands.description: System LO Commands (Special Function 42.0-42.7). AC=Auxiliary commands, PS=CW prefix/suffix, TM=Settling time in ms, MN=Minimum frequency in MHz, MX=Maximum frequency in MHz, PL=System LO power level, PC=Power prefix/suffix for custom LO, FC=Frequency prefix/suffix for custom LO. Follow value entries with EN.
commands.set: AC | PS | TM <ms> EN | MN <MHz> EN | MX <MHz> EN | PL <value> EN | PC | FC

commands.name: noise_figure_test_set_control
commands.description: Noise Figure Test Set Control (Special Function 45.0-45.2). TE=Enable test set in Modes 1.5-1.9, TS=Enable test set in all Modes (1.0-1.9), TD=Disable test set in all modes.

commands.name: test_set_fine_tuning_calibration
commands.description: Noise Figure Test Set YIG Filter Fine Tuning Calibration (Special Function 36.0-36.4). FT=fine tuning cal with measurement-system cal (warn E28 if needed), FD=disable fine tuning before cal, FW=disable fine tuning and warning E28, PF=fine tune from start to stop frequencies, FF=fine tune at current frequency.

commands.name: test_set_coarse_tuning_calibration
commands.description: Noise Figure Test Set YIG Filter Coarse Tuning Calibration (Special Function 64.0-64.1). CP=Enable coarse tuning calibration, CU=Select upper frequency of test set.

parameters.name: selection
parameters.range: CP=enable coarse cal, CU=select upper freq

commands.name: test_set_internal_sideband
commands.description: Noise Figure Test Set Internal Sideband Frequency Offset (Special Function 17.0-17.2). B4=Single sideband operation (Modes 1.5-1.9), B5=Double sideband operation (Modes 1.5-1.9), CF=Upper/lower sideband crossover frequency selection.

commands.name: internal_if_selection
commands.description: Noise Figure Measurement System Internal IF (Special Function 19.2-19.5). S2=Display/enter internal IF for SSB2, S3=internal IF for SSB3, S4=internal IF for DSB, S5=Display NFM input frequency. Defaults: SSB2=700, SSB3=450, DSB=25 MHz.
commands.set: S2 <MHz> EN | S3 <MHz> EN | S4 <MHz> EN | S5

parameters.name: if
parameters.range: SSB2 default 700, SSB3 default 450, DSB default 25

commands.name: sib_controller
commands.description: System Interface Bus (SIB) Controller (Special Function 48.0-48.2). SC=Enable NFM as System Controller on the SIB, NC=Disable NFM as System Controller, DC=Disable Error 48 (E48).

commands.name: system_lo_sib_control
commands.description: System Local Oscillator Control on the SIB (Special Function 46.0-46.1). LE=Enable system LO on the SIB, LD=Disable system LO on the SIB.

commands.name: plotter_control
commands.description: Plotter Control (Special Function 47.0-47.1). PI=Plotter is on the SIB, PM=Enable plot data to be read on the HP-IB.

parameters.name: state
parameters.range: PI=plotter on SIB, PM=plot data read on HP-IB

commands.name: sib_sharing_control
commands.description: Sharing Control on the SIB (Special Function 49.0-49.7). DP=Disable auto pass control, EP=Enable auto pass control, CR=Release control of SIB, CT=Take control of SIB, SB=Serial poll on SIB, DD=Selective device clear of pass-through device, DS=Device clear on SIB (if active controller), IS=Interface clear on SIB (if system controller).
commands.set: DP | EP | CR | CT | SB | DD | DS | IS

commands.name: scalar_analyzer_control
commands.description: HP 8757 Scalar Analyzer Control (Special Function 47.2-47.5). ZP=Enable plot to HP 8757, ZQ=Disable plot to HP 8757, ZR=Display/enter HP 8757 SIB address, ZS=Number of measurement frequencies per HP 8757 display refresh.
commands.set: ZP | ZQ | ZR <addr> EN | ZS <n> EN

commands.name: individual_rf_attenuator
commands.description: Individual RF Attenuator Selection (Special Function 63.0-63.5). Z0=Select RF thru path, Z1=Select 10 dB pad No.1, Z2=Select 20 dB input amplifier, Z4=Select 10 dB pad No.2, Z5=Select 10 dB pad No.3.

parameters.name: selection
parameters.range: Z0=thru, Z1=10dB#1, Z2=20dB amp, Z4=10dB#2, Z5=10dB#3

commands.name: display_test
commands.description: Enable Display Test (Special Function 91.0).

commands.name: keyboard_test
commands.description: Keyboard Test (Special Function 90.0-90.8). KY=Display key codes, K1-K8=Key test row 1 through row 8.

commands.name: recorder_test
commands.description: Recorder Test Functions (Special Function 82.0-82.2). A7=Enable recorder test, XV=X-axis test, YV=Y-axis test.
